revision.text: 白鳥神社（しらとりじんじゃ）は富山県富山市（旧越中国婦負郡）八尾町にある神社。
白鳥神社（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[越中国]][[婦負郡]]）[[八尾町]]にある神社。
白鳥神社（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[越中国]][[婦負郡]]）[[八尾町]]にある神社。 ==由緒== ==社名== ==祭神== ==歴史== ==行事・祭礼== ==交通==

白鳥神社（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[越中国]][[婦負郡]]）[[八尾町]]にある神社。
== 由緒 == == 社名 == == 祭神 == == 歴史 == == 行事・祭礼 == == 交通 == {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしや}} [[Category:富山市]] [[Category:富山県の神社]]

revision.comment: +bold
revision.text:
'''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[越中国]][[婦負郡]]）[[八尾町]]にある神社。
== 由緒 == == 社名 == == 祭神 == == 歴史 == == 行事・祭礼 == == 交通 == {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしや}} [[Category:富山市]] [[Category:富山県の神社]]

'''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[越中国]][[婦負郡]]）[[八尾町]]にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも白鳥神社（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]]
== 歴史 == この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、延喜式に記載された式内社とされる。
== 行事・祭礼 ==
==所在地== *富山県富山市八尾町三田1894
== 交通 ==
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしや}} [[Category:富山市]] [[Category:富山県の神社]]

'''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[越中国]][[婦負郡]]）[[八尾町]]にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも白鳥神社（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]]
== 歴史 == この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、延喜式に記載された式内社とされる。
== 行事・祭礼 ==
==所在地== *富山県富山市八尾町三田1894
== 交通 == *最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしや}} [[Category:富山市]] [[Category:富山県の神社]]

'''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[越中国]][[婦負郡]]）[[八尾町]]にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも白鳥神社（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]]
== 歴史 == この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、延喜式に記載された式内社とされる。
== 行事・祭礼 ==
==所在地== *富山県富山市八尾町三田1894
== 交通 == *最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅 (富山県)|千里駅]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしや}} [[Category:富山市]] [[Category:富山県の神社]]

'''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[越中国]][[婦負郡]]）[[八尾町]]にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも白鳥神社（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]]
== 歴史 == この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、延喜式に記載された式内社とされる。
== 行事・祭礼 ==
==所在地== *富山県富山市八尾町三田1894
== 交通 == *最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅 (富山県)|千里駅]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしやとやまし}} [[Category:富山市]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:郷社]]

{{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 白鳥神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[富山県]][[富山市]][[八尾町]]三田、 富山市寺町 |位置 = |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = 郷社 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[越中国]][[婦負郡]]）[[八尾町]]にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも白鳥神社（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市[[婦中町]]友坂の[[熊野神社]]が[[論社]]に比定されている。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]] *[[アメノヒボコ|比保古]]が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。
== 歴史 == [[仲哀天皇]]年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『[[日本書紀]]』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父[[日本武尊]]の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、[[越国]]から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。
この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された[[越中国]][[婦負郡]][[国幣小社]]とされる。
[[中世]]には、[[承暦]]4年（1080年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、[[康和]]5年（1103年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref> == 神階 == == 神紋 == *十六八重菊。（三田社） *三つ巴。（寺町社） == 建造物 == *三田社 **社殿 - 流造。 **拝殿 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿 == 文化財 == === 県指定 === == 行事・祭礼 == == 交通 == * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅]]、南西に徒歩35分。（三田社） * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[西富山駅]]、南西に徒歩10分。（寺町社） ==所在地== *富山県富山市八尾町三田字道円1894 *富山県富山市寺町字向田122 == 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[鈴鹿連胤]] 1902年『神社覈録』会通社 * [[黒板勝美]] 1999年『国史大系 第29巻上 朝野群載』吉川弘文館
== 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Shiratori-zinja}} *[[白鳥神社]] *[[ヤマトタケル]] *[[蘆髪蒲見別王]] *[[神社一覧]] *[[越中国]] == 外部リンク == *[[http://www.genbu.net/ 玄松子の記憶]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしやとやまし}} [[Category:富山市]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:越中国]] [[Category:郷社]]

{{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 白鳥神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[富山県]][[富山市]][[八尾町]]三田、 富山市寺町 |位置 = |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = 郷社 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[越中国]][[婦負郡]]）[[八尾町]]にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも白鳥神社（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市[[婦中町]]友坂の[[熊野神社]]が[[論社]]に比定されている。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]] *[[アメノヒボコ|比保古]]が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。
== 歴史 == [[仲哀天皇]]年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『[[日本書紀]]』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父[[日本武尊]]の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、[[越国]]から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。
この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された[[越中国]][[婦負郡]][[国幣小社]]とされる。
[[中世]]には、[[承暦]]4年（1080年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、[[康和]]5年（1103年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref> == 神階 == == 神紋 == *十六八重菊。（三田社） *三つ巴。（寺町社） == 建造物 == *三田社 **本殿 **拝殿 ‐流造。 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿 == 文化財 == === 県指定 === == 行事・祭礼 == == 交通 == * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅]]、南西に徒歩35分。（三田社） * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[西富山駅]]、南西に徒歩10分。（寺町社） ==所在地== *富山県富山市八尾町三田字道円1894 *富山県富山市寺町字向田122 == 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[鈴鹿連胤]] 1902年『[[神社覈録]]』会通社 * [[黒板勝美]] 1999年『[[国史大系]] 第29巻上 [[朝野群載]]』吉川弘文館
== 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Shiratori-zinja}} *[[白鳥神社]] *[[ヤマトタケル]] *[[蘆髪蒲見別王]] *[[神社一覧]] *[[越中国]] == 外部リンク == *[[http://www.genbu.net/ 玄松子の記憶]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしやとやまし}} [[Category:富山市]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:越中国]] [[Category:郷社]]

{{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 白鳥神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町三田1894（三田社）
富山県富山市寺町字向田122（寺町社） 富山市寺町 |位置 = |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = 郷社（三田社）、村社（寺町社） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも白鳥神社（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の[[熊野神社]]が[[論社]]に比定されている。 == 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]] *[[アメノヒボコ|比保古]]が祭神であるとの考証もされている鈴鹿（1902年）518p。 == 歴史 == [[仲哀天皇]]年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『[[日本書紀]]』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父[[日本武尊]]の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、[[越国]]から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。 この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された[[越中国]][[婦負郡]][[国幣小社]]とされる。 [[中世]]には、1080年（[[承暦]]4年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、1103年（[[康和]]5年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。黒板（1999年）137-139p == 神紋 == *十六八重菊（三田社） *三つ巴（寺町社） == 建造物 == *三田社 **本殿 **拝殿 - 流造 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿 == 交通 == * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅 (富山県)|千里駅]]、南西に徒歩35分（三田社） * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[西富山駅]]、南西に徒歩10分（寺町社） == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * [[鈴鹿連胤]] 1902年『[[神社覈録]]』会通社　 * [[黒板勝美]] 1999年『[[国史大系]] 第29巻上 [[朝野群載]]』吉川弘文館 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Shiratori-zinja}} *[[白鳥神社]] *[[ヤマトタケル]] *[[蘆髪蒲見別王]] *[[神社一覧]] *[[越中国]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.genbu.net/ 玄松子の記憶] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしやとやまし}} [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:越中国]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:富山県の旧村社]]

富山県富山市寺町字向田122（寺町社） |位置 = |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = 郷社（三田社）
村社（寺町社） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも白鳥神社（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の[[熊野神社]]が[[論社]]に比定されている。 == 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]] *[[アメノヒボコ|比保古]]が祭神であるとの考証もされている鈴鹿（1902年）518p。 == 歴史 == [[仲哀天皇]]年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『[[日本書紀]]』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父[[日本武尊]]の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、[[越国]]から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。 この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された[[越中国]][[婦負郡]][[国幣小社]]とされる。 [[中世]]には、1080年（[[承暦]]4年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、1103年（[[康和]]5年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。黒板（1999年）137-139p == 神紋 == *十六八重菊（三田社） *三つ巴（寺町社） == 建造物 == *三田社 **本殿 **拝殿 - 流造 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿 == 交通 == * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅 (富山県)|千里駅]]、南西に徒歩35分（三田社） * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[西富山駅]]、南西に徒歩10分（寺町社） == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * [[鈴鹿連胤]] 1902年『[[神社覈録]]』会通社　 * [[黒板勝美]] 1999年『[[国史大系]] 第29巻上 [[朝野群載]]』吉川弘文館 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Shiratori-zinja}} *[[白鳥神社]] *[[ヤマトタケル]] *[[蘆髪蒲見別王]] *[[神社一覧]] *[[越中国]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしやとやまし}} [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:富山県の旧村社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル]]

富山県富山市寺町字向田122（寺町社） |位置 = |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = 郷社（三田社）
村社（寺町社） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも白鳥神社（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の[[熊野神社]]が[[論社]]に比定されている。 == 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]] *[[アメノヒボコ|比保古]]が祭神であるとの考証もされている鈴鹿（1902年）518p。 == 歴史 == [[仲哀天皇]]年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『[[日本書紀]]』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父[[日本武尊]]の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、[[越国]]から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。 この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された[[越中国]][[婦負郡]][[国幣小社]]とされる。 [[中世]]には、1080年（[[承暦]]4年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、1103年（[[康和]]5年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。黒板（1999年）137-139p == 神紋 == *十六八重菊（三田社） *三つ巴（寺町社） == 建造物 == *三田社 **本殿 **拝殿 - 流造 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿 == 交通 == * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅 (富山県)|千里駅]]、南西に徒歩35分（三田社） * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[西富山駅]]、南西に徒歩10分（寺町社） == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * [[鈴鹿連胤]] 1902年『[[神社覈録]]』会通社　 * [[黒板勝美]] 1999年『[[国史大系]] 第29巻上 [[朝野群載]]』吉川弘文館 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Shiratori-zinja}} *[[白鳥神社]] *[[ヤマトタケル]] *[[蘆髪蒲見別王]] *[[神社一覧]] *[[越中国]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしやとやまし}} [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:富山県の旧村社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル]]

{{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 白鳥神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=富山市}} |所在地 = [[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町三田1894 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=白鳥神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも[[白鳥神社 (富山市寺町)|白鳥神社]]（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の[[熊野神社]]が[[論社]]に比定されている。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]] *[[アメノヒボコ|比保古]]が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。
== 歴史 == [[仲哀天皇]]年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『[[日本書紀]]』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父[[日本武尊]]の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、[[越国]]から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。
この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された[[越中国]][[婦負郡]][[国幣小社]]とされる。
[[中世]]には、1080年（[[承暦]]4年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、1103年（[[康和]]5年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref>
== 神紋 == *十六八重菊（三田社） *三つ巴（寺町社）
== 建造物 == *三田社 **本殿 **拝殿 - 流造 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿
== 交通 == * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅 (富山県)|千里駅]]、南西に徒歩35分（三田社） * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[西富山駅]]、南西に徒歩10分（寺町社）
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[鈴鹿連胤]] 1902年『[[神社覈録]]』会通社 * [[黒板勝美]] 1999年『[[国史大系]] 第29巻上 [[朝野群載]]』吉川弘文館
== 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Shiratori-zinja}} *[[白鳥神社]] *[[ヤマトタケル]] *[[蘆髪蒲見別王]] *[[神社一覧]] *[[越中国]]

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

revision.comment: 22 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 白鳥神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=富山市}} |所在地 = [[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町三田1894 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=白鳥神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも[[白鳥神社 (富山市寺町)|白鳥神社]]（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の[[熊野神社]]が[[論社]]に比定されている。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]] *[[アメノヒボコ|比保古]]が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。
== 歴史 == [[仲哀天皇]]年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『[[日本書紀]]』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父[[日本武尊]]の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、[[越国]]から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。
この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された[[越中国]][[婦負郡]][[国幣小社]]とされる。
[[中世]]には、1080年（[[承暦]]4年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、1103年（[[康和]]5年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref>
== 神紋 == *十六八重菊（三田社） *三つ巴（寺町社）
== 建造物 == *三田社 **本殿 **拝殿 - 流造 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿
== 交通 == * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅 (富山県)|千里駅]]、南西に徒歩35分（三田社） * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[西富山駅]]、南西に徒歩10分（寺町社）
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[鈴鹿連胤]] 1902年『[[神社覈録]]』会通社 * [[黒板勝美]] 1999年『[[国史大系]] 第29巻上 [[朝野群載]]』吉川弘文館
== 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Shiratori-zinja}} *[[白鳥神社]] *[[ヤマトタケル]] *[[蘆髪蒲見別王]] *[[神社一覧]] *[[越中国]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりしんしやとやまし}} [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:富山県の旧村社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル]]

[[中世]]には、1080年（[[承暦]]4年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、1103年（[[康和]]5年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref>

== 建造物 == *三田社 **本殿 **拝殿 - 流造 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿

== 脚注 == <references/>

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

[[JA:白鳥神社 (富山市)]]
{{wikidata link|Q11580592}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|白鳥神社 (富山市)|version=107329842|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 白鳥神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=富山市}} |所在地 = [[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町三田1894 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=白鳥神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも[[白鳥神社 (富山市寺町)|白鳥神社]]（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の[[熊野神社]]が[[論社]]に比定されている。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]] *[[アメノヒボコ|比保古]]が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。
== 歴史 == [[仲哀天皇]]年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『[[日本書紀]]』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父[[日本武尊]]の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、[[越国]]から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。
この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された[[越中国]][[婦負郡]][[国幣小社]]とされる。
[[中世]]には、1080年（[[承暦]]4年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、1103年（[[康和]]5年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref>
== 神紋 == *十六八重菊（三田社） *三つ巴（寺町社）
== 建造物 == *三田社 **本殿 **拝殿 - 流造 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿
== 交通 == * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅 (富山県)|千里駅]]、南西に徒歩35分（三田社） * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[西富山駅]]、南西に徒歩10分（寺町社）
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[鈴鹿連胤]] 1902年『[[神社覈録]]』会通社 * [[黒板勝美]] 1999年『[[国史大系]] 第29巻上 [[朝野群載]]』吉川弘文館
== 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Shiratori-zinja}} *[[白鳥神社]] *[[ヤマトタケル]] *[[蘆髪蒲見別王]] *[[神社一覧]] *[[越中国]]

==End Japanese==

[[JA:白鳥神社 (富山市)]]
{{wikidata link|Q11580592}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|白鳥神社 (富山市)|version=107329842|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 白鳥神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=富山市}} |所在地 = [[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町三田1894 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=白鳥神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも[[白鳥神社 (富山市寺町)|白鳥神社]]（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の[[熊野神社]]が[[論社]]に比定されている。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]] *[[アメノヒボコ|比保古]]が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。
== 歴史 == [[仲哀天皇]]年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『[[日本書紀]]』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父[[日本武尊]]の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、[[越国]]から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。
この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された[[越中国]][[婦負郡]][[国幣小社]]とされる。
[[中世]]には、1080年（[[承暦]]4年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、1103年（[[康和]]5年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref>
== 神紋 == *十六八重菊（三田社） *三つ巴（寺町社）
== 建造物 == *三田社 **本殿 **拝殿 - 流造 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿
== 交通 == * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅 (富山県)|千里駅]]、南西に徒歩35分（三田社） * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[西富山駅]]、南西に徒歩10分（寺町社）
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[鈴鹿連胤]] 1902年『[[神社覈録]]』会通社 * [[黒板勝美]] 1999年『[[国史大系]] 第29巻上 [[朝野群載]]』吉川弘文館
== 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Shiratori-zinja}} *[[白鳥神社]] *[[ヤマトタケル]] *[[蘆髪蒲見別王]] *[[神社一覧]] *[[越中国]]
{{shinto-stub}}

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 白鳥神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=富山市}} |所在地 = [[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町三田1894 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=白鳥神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は[[富山県]][[富山市]]八尾町にある神社。[[延喜式]]内社とされ、[[社格]]は旧[[郷社]]。富山市寺町にも[[白鳥神社 (富山市寺町)|白鳥神社]]（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の[[熊野神社]]が[[論社]]に比定されている。
== 祭神 == *[[日本武尊]] *[[アメノヒボコ|比保古]]が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。
== 歴史 == [[仲哀天皇]]年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『[[日本書紀]]』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父[[日本武尊]]の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、[[越国]]から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。
この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された[[越中国]][[婦負郡]][[国幣小社]]とされる。
[[中世]]には、1080年（[[承暦]]4年）6月10日、越中国では[[鵜坂神社|鵜坂]]・[[気多神社（氷見市）|気多]]・[[三宅]]の諸社と共に、 [[卜部兼宗]]による[[亀卜]]・[[御体御卜]]が奏上され、1103年（[[康和]]5年）6月10日にも、[[高瀬神社|高瀬]]・[[林神社（砺波市）|林]]・鵜坂・[[速川神社|速川]]の諸社と共に、[[卜部兼良]]により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref>
== 神紋 == *十六八重菊（三田社） *三つ巴（寺町社）
== 建造物 == *三田社 **本殿 **拝殿 - 流造 *寺町社 **本殿 **拝殿
== 交通 == * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[千里駅 (富山県)|千里駅]]、南西に徒歩35分（三田社） * 最寄駅：JR[[高山本線]][[西富山駅]]、南西に徒歩10分（寺町社）
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[鈴鹿連胤]] 1902年『[[神社覈録]]』会通社 * [[黒板勝美]] 1999年『[[国史大系]] 第29巻上 [[朝野群載]]』吉川弘文館
== 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Shiratori-zinja}} *[[白鳥神社]] *[[ヤマトタケル]] *[[蘆髪蒲見別王]] *[[神社一覧]] *[[越中国]]
{{shinto-stub}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:富山県の旧村社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル]]

'''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は{{jalink|富山県|Q132929}}{{jalink|富山市|Q204266}}八尾町にある神社。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}内社とされ、{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は旧{{jalink|郷社}}。富山市寺町にも{{jalink|白鳥神社 (富山市寺町)||白鳥神社}}（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の{{jalink|熊野神社|Q3200355}}が{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}に比定されている。

== 祭神 ==
*{{jalink|日本武尊}}
*{{jalink|アメノヒボコ|Q713100|比保古}}が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。

== 歴史 ==
{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}}年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父{{jalink|日本武尊}}の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、{{jalink|越国|Q1351611}}から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。

この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された{{jalink|越中国|Q906899}}{{jalink|婦負郡|Q6987940}}{{jalink|国幣小社|Q9685496}}とされる。

{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}には、1080年（{{jalink|承暦|Q279743}}4年）6月10日、越中国では{{jalink|鵜坂神社|Q11675698|鵜坂}}・{{jalink|気多神社（氷見市）||気多}}・{{jalink|三宅|Q566667}}の諸社と共に、
{{jalink|卜部兼宗}}による{{jalink|亀卜|Q60989829}}・{{jalink|御体御卜}}が奏上され、1103年（{{jalink|康和|Q1193890}}5年）6月10日にも、{{jalink|高瀬神社|Q3791906|高瀬}}・{{jalink|林神社（砺波市）||林}}・鵜坂・{{jalink|速川神社|Q11639669|速川}}の諸社と共に、{{jalink|卜部兼良}}により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}}
{{神社
|名称   = 白鳥神社
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=富山市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|富山県|Q132929}}{{jalink|富山市|Q204266}}八尾町三田1894
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|ヤマトタケル|Q461258|日本武尊}}
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
|本殿   =

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は{{jalink|富山県|Q132929}}{{jalink|富山市|Q204266}}八尾町にある神社。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}内社とされ、{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。富山市寺町にも{{jalink|白鳥神社 (富山市寺町)||白鳥神社}}（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の{{jalink|熊野神社|Q3200355}}が{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}に比定されている。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|日本武尊}}
*{{jalink|アメノヒボコ|Q713100|比保古}}が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。

=== 歴史 ===
{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}}年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父{{jalink|日本武尊}}の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、{{jalink|越国|Q1351611}}から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。

この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された{{jalink|越中国|Q906899}}{{jalink|婦負郡|Q6987940}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|Q11064932|国幣小社}}とされる。

{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}には、1080年（{{jalink|承暦|Q279743}}4年）6月10日、越中国では{{jalink|鵜坂神社|Q11675698|鵜坂}}・{{jalink|気多神社（氷見市）||気多}}・{{jalink|三宅|Q566667}}の諸社と共に、
{{jalink|卜部兼宗}}による{{jalink|亀卜|Q60989829}}・{{jalink|御体御卜}}が奏上され、1103年（{{jalink|康和|Q1193890}}5年）6月10日にも、{{jalink|高瀬神社|Q3791906|高瀬}}・{{jalink|林神社（砺波市）||林}}・鵜坂・{{jalink|速川神社|Q11639669|速川}}の諸社と共に、{{jalink|卜部兼良}}により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref>

=== 神紋 ===
*十六八重菊（三田社）
*三つ巴（寺町社）

* 最寄駅：JR{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|西富山駅|Q11627677}}、南西に徒歩10分（寺町社）

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}}

'''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は{{jalink|富山県|Q132929}}{{jalink|富山市|Q204266}}八尾町にある神社。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}内社とされ、{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。富山市寺町にも{{jalink|白鳥神社 (富山市寺町)||白鳥神社}}（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の{{jalink|熊野神社|Q3200355}}が{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}に比定されている。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|日本武尊}}
*{{jalink|アメノヒボコ|Q713100|比保古}}が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。

=== 歴史 ===
{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}}年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父{{jalink|日本武尊}}の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、{{jalink|越国|Q1351611}}から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。

この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された{{jalink|越中国|Q906899}}{{jalink|婦負郡|Q6987940}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|Q11064932|国幣小社}}とされる。

{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}には、1080年（{{jalink|承暦|Q279743}}4年）6月10日、越中国では{{jalink|鵜坂神社|Q11675698|鵜坂}}・{{jalink|気多神社（氷見市）||気多}}・{{jalink|三宅|Q566667}}の諸社と共に、
{{jalink|卜部兼宗}}による{{jalink|亀卜|Q60989829}}・{{jalink|御体御卜}}が奏上され、1103年（{{jalink|康和|Q1193890}}5年）6月10日にも、{{jalink|高瀬神社|Q3791906|高瀬}}・{{jalink|林神社（砺波市）||林}}・鵜坂・{{jalink|速川神社|Q11639669|速川}}の諸社と共に、{{jalink|卜部兼良}}により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

[[JA:白鳥神社 (富山市)]]

{{wikidata link|Q11580592}}

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|富山県富山市の白鳥神社|全国の白鳥神社|白鳥神社}}
{{神社
|名称   = 白鳥神社
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=富山市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|富山県|Q132929}}{{jalink|富山市|Q204266}}八尾町三田1894
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|ヤマトタケル|Q461258|日本武尊}}
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   =
|神事   =
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=白鳥神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''白鳥神社'''（しらとりじんじゃ）は{{jalink|富山県|Q132929}}{{jalink|富山市|Q204266}}八尾町にある神社。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}内社とされ、{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。富山市寺町にも{{jalink|白鳥神社 (富山市寺町)||白鳥神社}}（社格は旧村社）があり、この神社と関係があると伝えられる。他に同市婦中町友坂の{{jalink|熊野神社|Q3200355}}が{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}に比定されている。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|日本武尊}}
*{{jalink|アメノヒボコ|Q713100|比保古}}が祭神であるとの考証もされている<ref>鈴鹿（1902年）518p</ref>。

=== 歴史 ===
{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}}年間（192年-200年）の勧請とする説がある。『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』には、仲哀天皇元年（192年）11月、天皇が、白鳥となった父{{jalink|日本武尊}}の神霊を慕い、白鳥の献上を諸国に命じた結果、閏の11月4日、{{jalink|越国|Q1351611}}から4隻の白鳥が献上されたとの記載があり、越中国の当地の白鳥が献上されたとする。

この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に'''白鳥神社'''として記載された{{jalink|越中国|Q906899}}{{jalink|婦負郡|Q6987940}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|Q11064932|国幣小社}}とされる。

{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}には、1080年（{{jalink|承暦|Q279743}}4年）6月10日、越中国では{{jalink|鵜坂神社|Q11675698|鵜坂}}・{{jalink|気多神社（氷見市）||気多}}・{{jalink|三宅|Q566667}}の諸社と共に、
{{jalink|卜部兼宗}}による{{jalink|亀卜|Q60989829}}・{{jalink|御体御卜}}が奏上され、1103年（{{jalink|康和|Q1193890}}5年）6月10日にも、{{jalink|高瀬神社|Q3791906|高瀬}}・{{jalink|林神社（砺波市）||林}}・鵜坂・{{jalink|速川神社|Q11639669|速川}}の諸社と共に、{{jalink|卜部兼良}}により同様の奏上が行われている。<ref>黒板（1999年）137-139p</ref>

=== 神紋 ===
*十六八重菊（三田社）
*三つ巴（寺町社）

* 最寄駅：JR{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|西富山駅|Q11627677}}、南西に徒歩10分（寺町社）

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|Shiratori Shrine in Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture|Shiratori Shrines nationwide|Shiratori Shrine}}

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}}, {{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, and its {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. There is also a {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)||Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{jalink|Kumano Shrine|Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{jalink|Prince Yamato Takeru}}
* It has also been proposed that {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{jalink|Prince Yamato Takeru}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{jalink|Echigo Province|Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' as a {{jalink|Etchū Province|Q906899}} {{jalink|Nei District|Q6987940}} {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{jalink|Jōryaku|Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{jalink|Urabe Kanemune}} using {{jalink|kiboku|Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{jalink|Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|Usaka}}, {{jalink|Keta Shrine (Himi City)||Keta}}, and {{jalink|Miyake|Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{jalink|Kōwa|Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{jalink|Urabe Kaneyoshi}} together with {{jalink|Takase Shrine|Q3791906|Takase}}, {{jalink|Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)||Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{jalink|Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}} {{jalink|Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}} {{jalink|Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|Shiratori Shrine in Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture|Shiratori Shrines nationwide|Shiratori Shrine}}
{{Shrine

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}}, {{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, and its {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. There is also a {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)||Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{jalink|Kumano Shrine|Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{jalink|Prince Yamato Takeru}}
* It has also been proposed that {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{jalink|Prince Yamato Takeru}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{jalink|Echigo Province|Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' as a {{jalink|Etchū Province|Q906899}} {{jalink|Nei District|Q6987940}} {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{jalink|Jōryaku|Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{jalink|Urabe Kanemune}} using {{jalink|kiboku|Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{jalink|Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|Usaka}}, {{jalink|Keta Shrine (Himi City)||Keta}}, and {{jalink|Miyake|Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{jalink|Kōwa|Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{jalink|Urabe Kaneyoshi}} together with {{jalink|Takase Shrine|Q3791906|Takase}}, {{jalink|Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)||Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{jalink|Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}} {{jalink|Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}} {{jalink|Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}}, {{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, and its {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. There is also a {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)||Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{jalink|Kumano Shrine|Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{jalink|Prince Yamato Takeru}}
* It has also been proposed that {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{jalink|Prince Yamato Takeru}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{jalink|Echigo Province|Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' as a {{jalink|Etchū Province|Q906899}} {{jalink|Nei District|Q6987940}} {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{jalink|Jōryaku|Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{jalink|Urabe Kanemune}} using {{jalink|kiboku|Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{jalink|Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|Usaka}}, {{jalink|Keta Shrine (Himi City)||Keta}}, and {{jalink|Miyake|Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{jalink|Kōwa|Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{jalink|Urabe Kaneyoshi}} together with {{jalink|Takase Shrine|Q3791906|Takase}}, {{jalink|Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)||Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{jalink|Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}} {{jalink|Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}} {{jalink|Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}}, {{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, and its {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. There is also a {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)||Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{jalink|Kumano Shrine|Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{jalink|Prince Yamato Takeru}}
* It has also been proposed that {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{jalink|Prince Yamato Takeru}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{jalink|Echigo Province|Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' as a {{jalink|Etchū Province|Q906899}} {{jalink|Nei District|Q6987940}} {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{jalink|Jōryaku|Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{jalink|Urabe Kanemune}} using {{jalink|kiboku|Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{jalink|Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|Usaka}}, {{jalink|Keta Shrine (Himi City)||Keta}}, and {{jalink|Miyake|Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{jalink|Kōwa|Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{jalink|Urabe Kaneyoshi}} together with {{jalink|Takase Shrine|Q3791906|Takase}}, {{jalink|Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)||Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{jalink|Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}} {{jalink|Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}} {{jalink|Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}}, {{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, and its {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. There is also a {{jalink|Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)||Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{jalink|Kumano Shrine|Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{jalink|Prince Yamato Takeru}}
* It has also been proposed that {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{jalink|Prince Yamato Takeru}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{jalink|Echigo Province|Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' as a {{jalink|Etchū Province|Q906899}} {{jalink|Nei District|Q6987940}} {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{jalink|Jōryaku|Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{jalink|Urabe Kanemune}} using {{jalink|kiboku|Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{jalink|Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|Usaka}}, {{jalink|Keta Shrine (Himi City)||Keta}}, and {{jalink|Miyake|Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{jalink|Kōwa|Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{jalink|Urabe Kaneyoshi}} together with {{jalink|Takase Shrine|Q3791906|Takase}}, {{jalink|Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)||Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{jalink|Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

** Worship hall

=== Access ===

* Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}} {{jalink|Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|Shiratori Shrine in Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture|Shiratori Shrines nationwide|Shiratori Shrine}}
{{Shrine

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, and its {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. There is also a {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)||WD=|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill||lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|UNKNOWN}}
* It has also been proposed that {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{ill||lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|UNKNOWN}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' as a {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899}} {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Jōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{ill||lt=Urabe Kanemune|UNKNOWN}} using {{ill|lt=kiboku|Q60989829|WD=Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{ill|lt=Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|WD=Q11675698|lt=Usaka}}, {{ill|lt=Keta Shrine (Himi City)||WD=|lt=Keta}}, and {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q566667|WD=Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Kōwa|Q1193890|WD=Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{ill||lt=Urabe Kaneyoshi|UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Takase Shrine|Q3791906|WD=Q3791906|lt=Takase}}, {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)||WD=|lt=Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{ill|lt=Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|WD=Q11639669|lt=Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|WD=Q5102058|lt=Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677|WD=Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, and its {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. There is also a {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)||WD=|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|UNKNOWN}}
* It has also been proposed that {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|UNKNOWN}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' as a {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899}} {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Jōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kanemune|UNKNOWN}} using {{ill|lt=kiboku|Q60989829|WD=Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{ill|lt=Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|WD=Q11675698|lt=Usaka}}, {{ill|lt=Keta Shrine (Himi City)||WD=|lt=Keta}}, and {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q566667|WD=Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Kōwa|Q1193890|WD=Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kaneyoshi|UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Takase Shrine|Q3791906|WD=Q3791906|lt=Takase}}, {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)||WD=|lt=Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{ill|lt=Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|WD=Q11639669|lt=Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|WD=Q5102058|lt=Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677|WD=Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|Shiratori Shrine in Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture|Shiratori Shrines nationwide|Shiratori Shrine}}
{{Shrine
|Name   = Shiratori Shrine
|Image  = {{Image requested|cat=Toyama City}}
|Location = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}}1894 Mita, Yatsuo-machi
|Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, and its {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. There is also a {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)||WD=|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}
* It has also been proposed that {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' as a {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899}} {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Jōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kanemune|1=UNKNOWN}} using {{ill|lt=kiboku|Q60989829|WD=Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{ill|lt=Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|WD=Q11675698|lt=Usaka}}, {{ill|lt=Keta Shrine (Himi City)||WD=|lt=Keta}}, and {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q566667|WD=Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Kōwa|Q1193890|WD=Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kaneyoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Takase Shrine|Q3791906|WD=Q3791906|lt=Takase}}, {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)||WD=|lt=Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{ill|lt=Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|WD=Q11639669|lt=Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|WD=Q5102058|lt=Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677|WD=Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|Shiratori Shrine in Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture|Shiratori Shrines nationwide|Shiratori Shrine}}
{{Shrine
|Name   = Shiratori Shrine
|Image  = {{Image requested|cat=Toyama City}}
|Location = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}}1894 Mita, Yatsuo-machi
|Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, and its {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. There is also a {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}
* It has also been proposed that {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' as a {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899}} {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Jōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kanemune|1=UNKNOWN}} using {{ill|lt=kiboku|Q60989829|WD=Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{ill|lt=Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|WD=Q11675698|lt=Usaka}}, {{ill|lt=Keta Shrine (Himi City)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Keta}}, and {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q566667|WD=Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Kōwa|Q1193890|WD=Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kaneyoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Takase Shrine|Q3791906|WD=Q3791906|lt=Takase}}, {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{ill|lt=Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|WD=Q11639669|lt=Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|WD=Q5102058|lt=Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677|WD=Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|Shiratori Shrine in Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture|Shiratori Shrines nationwide|Shiratori Shrine}}

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, and its {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. There is also a {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}
* It has also been proposed that {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' as a {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899}} {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Jōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kanemune|1=UNKNOWN}} using {{ill|lt=kiboku|Q60989829|WD=Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{ill|lt=Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|WD=Q11675698|lt=Usaka}}, {{ill|lt=Keta Shrine (Himi City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Keta}}, and {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q566667|WD=Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Kōwa|Q1193890|WD=Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kaneyoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Takase Shrine|Q3791906|WD=Q3791906|lt=Takase}}, {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{ill|lt=Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|WD=Q11639669|lt=Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===

** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|WD=Q5102058|lt=Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677|WD=Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|Shiratori Shrine in Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture|Shiratori Shrines nationwide|Shiratori Shrine}}
{{Shrine

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, and its {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. There is also a {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}
* It has also been proposed that {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' as a {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899}} {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Jōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kanemune|1=UNKNOWN}} using {{ill|lt=kiboku|Q60989829|WD=Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{ill|lt=Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|WD=Q11675698|lt=Usaka}}, {{ill|lt=Keta Shrine (Himi City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Keta}}, and {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q566667|WD=Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Kōwa|Q1193890|WD=Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kaneyoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Takase Shrine|Q3791906|WD=Q3791906|lt=Takase}}, {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{ill|lt=Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|WD=Q11639669|lt=Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|WD=Q5102058|lt=Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677|WD=Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{otheruses|Shiratori Shrine in Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture|Shiratori Shrines nationwide|Shiratori Shrine}}
{{Shrine

|Image  = {{Image requested|cat=Toyama City}}

|Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru}}

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, and its {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was formerly that of a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. There is also a {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}
* It has also been proposed that {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Hiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} (192–200). According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' as a {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899}} {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)}}.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Jōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kanemune|1=UNKNOWN}} using {{ill|lt=kiboku|Q60989829|WD=Q60989829}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{ill|lt=Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|WD=Q11675698|lt=Usaka}}, {{ill|lt=Keta Shrine (Himi City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Keta}}, and {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q566667|WD=Q566667}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Kōwa|Q1193890|WD=Q1193890}}), the same type of report was made by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kaneyoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Takase Shrine|Q3791906|WD=Q3791906|lt=Takase}}, {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{ill|lt=Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|WD=Q11639669|lt=Hayakawa}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|WD=Q5102058|lt=Chisato Station}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}} {{ill|lt=Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677|WD=Q11627677}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, and its {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was formerly that of a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. There is also a {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355|1=Kumano shrine}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}
* It has also been proposed that {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Hiboko|1=Amenohiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} (192–200). According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' as a {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899|1=Etchū Province}} {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940|1=Nei District, Toyama}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Jōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743|1=Jōryaku}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kanemune|1=UNKNOWN}} using {{ill|lt=kiboku|Q60989829|WD=Q60989829|1=kiboku}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{ill|lt=Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|WD=Q11675698|lt=Usaka|1=Usaka Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Keta Shrine (Himi City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Keta}}, and {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q566667|WD=Q566667|1=Miyake}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Kōwa|Q1193890|WD=Q1193890|1=Kōwa (Heian period)}}), the same type of report was made by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kaneyoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Takase Shrine|Q3791906|WD=Q3791906|lt=Takase|1=Takase Shrine (Nanto)}}, {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{ill|lt=Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|WD=Q11639669|lt=Hayakawa|1=Hayakawa Shrine}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Yatsuo-machi, {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is considered a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, and its {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was formerly that of a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. There is also a {{ill|lt=Shiratori Shrine (Teramachi, Toyama City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiratori Shrine}} in Teramachi, Toyama City (ranked as a former village shrine), which is said to be related to this shrine. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355|1=Kumano shrine}} in Tomosaka, Fuchū-machi of the same city is identified as a {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (candidate shrine).

=== Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}
* It has also been proposed that {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Hiboko|1=Amenohiboko}} is enshrined here<ref>Suzuka (1902), p. 518</ref>.

=== History ===
There is a theory that the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} (192–200). According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}', in the 11th month of the first year of Emperor Chūai (192), the emperor, longing for the divine spirit of his father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}, who had become a white bird, ordered provinces throughout the country to present white birds. As a result, on the intercalary 11th month, 4th day, four white birds were presented from {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}}, and it is said that the white birds from this locality in Etchū Province were among those presented.

Although the exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown, it is identified with the '''Shiratori Shrine''' listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' as a {{ill|lt=Etchū Province|Q906899|WD=Q906899|1=Etchū Province}} {{ill|lt=Nei District|Q6987940|WD=Q6987940|1=Nei District, Toyama}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō#Shrine ranks|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=minor national shrine (Kokuhei-shōsha)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, on June 10, 1080 (the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Jōryaku|Q279743|WD=Q279743|1=Jōryaku}}), in Etchū Province, divinations by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kanemune|1=UNKNOWN}} using {{ill|lt=kiboku|Q60989829|WD=Q60989829|1=kiboku}} and sacred body divination were reported together with those of {{ill|lt=Usaka Shrine|Q11675698|WD=Q11675698|lt=Usaka|1=Usaka Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Keta Shrine (Himi City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Keta}}, and {{ill|lt=Miyake|Q566667|WD=Q566667|1=Miyake}} shrines. Similarly, on June 10, 1103 (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Kōwa|Q1193890|WD=Q1193890|1=Kōwa (Heian period)}}), the same type of report was made by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Urabe Kaneyoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Takase Shrine|Q3791906|WD=Q3791906|lt=Takase|1=Takase Shrine (Nanto)}}, {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi}}, Usaka, and {{ill|lt=Hayakawa Shrine|Q11639669|WD=Q11639669|lt=Hayakawa|1=Hayakawa Shrine}} shrines.<ref>Kuroita (1999), pp. 137–139</ref>

=== Shrine Crests ===
* Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum (Mita Shrine)
* Triple tomoe (Teramachi Shrine)

=== Buildings ===
* Mita Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall – Nagare-zukuri style
* Teramachi Shrine
** Main hall
** Worship hall

=== Access ===
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Chisato Station (Toyama Prefecture)|Q5102058|WD=Q5102058|lt=Chisato Station|1=Chisato Station (Toyama)}}, 35-minute walk southwest (Mita Shrine)
* Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Nishi-Toyama Station|Q11627677|WD=Q11627677|1=Nishi-Toyama Station}}, 10-minute walk southwest (Teramachi Shrine)

{{nihongo|'''Shiratori Shrine (Toyama)'''|白鳥神社 (富山市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

== Japanese content ==
